title: periploca calophylla subsp. floribunda
intro: Periploca calophylla subsp. floribunda, also known as the Floribundous Yellow Milk Root or Yellow Milk Root, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Periploca calophylla subsp. floribunda include the potential treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a diuretic. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
